title: Закон за електронната търговия (ЗЕТ)
description: Bulgaria's e-commerce law implementing Directive 2000/31/EC on electronic commerce. Regulates information society services, establishes requirements for service providers, electronic contracts, intermediary liability, and commercial communications.

provisions.title: Subject and scope
provisions.content: This Act regulates the conditions and the procedure for the provision of information society services, including electronic commerce. This Act implements the requirements of Directive 2000/31/EC of the European Parliament and of the Council of 8 June 2000 on certain legal aspects of information society services, in particular electronic commerce, in the Internal Market (Directive on Electronic Commerce).

provisions.title: Definitions
provisions.content: For the purposes of this Act: 'information society service' means any service normally provided for remuneration, at a distance, by electronic means and at the individual request of a recipient of services; 'service provider' means any natural or legal person providing an information society service; 'recipient of services' means any natural or legal person who, for professional purposes or otherwise, uses an information society service.

provisions.title: Exclusions
provisions.content: This Act shall not apply to: the field of taxation; questions relating to the processing of personal data covered by the Personal Data Protection Act; activities of notaries or equivalent professions to the extent that they involve a direct and specific connection with the exercise of public authority; the representation of a client and defence of his interests before courts.

provisions.title: General information obligation
provisions.content: A service provider shall make accessible to the recipients of the service and to the competent authorities at least the following information: the name of the service provider; the geographic address at which the service provider is established; details of the service provider including electronic mail address; the trade register and registration number; details of the relevant supervisory authority where the activity is subject to an authorisation scheme; the VAT identification number.

provisions.title: Commercial communications
provisions.content: Commercial communications forming part of or constituting an information society service shall clearly be identifiable as such. The natural or legal person on whose behalf the commercial communication is made shall be clearly identifiable. Unsolicited commercial communications by electronic mail shall be identifiable clearly and unambiguously as such as soon as they are received by the recipient.

provisions.title: Pre-contractual information
provisions.content: Before the conclusion of a contract by electronic means, the service provider shall provide the recipient of services with at least the following information: the different technical steps to follow to conclude the contract; whether or not the concluded contract will be stored by the service provider and whether it will be accessible; the technical means for identifying and correcting input errors; the languages offered for the conclusion of the contract.

provisions.title: Validity of electronic contracts
provisions.content: A contract may be concluded by electronic means. The validity of electronic contracts shall not be denied solely on the grounds that the contract has been concluded by electronic means. Where the law requires a written form, an electronic document meeting the requirements of the Electronic Document and Electronic Certification Services Act shall satisfy that requirement.

provisions.title: Order acknowledgement
provisions.content: The service provider shall acknowledge the receipt of the order of the recipient of services without undue delay and by electronic means. The order and the acknowledgement of receipt are deemed to be received when the parties to whom they are addressed are able to access them. The service provider shall make available to the recipient of services appropriate, effective and accessible technical means allowing him to identify and correct input errors prior to the placing of the order.

provisions.title: Consumer protection in e-commerce
provisions.content: Consumers concluding contracts by electronic means shall have the same rights as those provided under the Consumer Protection Act, including the right of withdrawal within 14 days without giving any reason and without incurring any costs other than the direct cost of returning the goods. The service provider shall inform the consumer of the right of withdrawal and provide a model withdrawal form.

provisions.chapter: Chapter 4 - Liability of Intermediary Service Providers
provisions.title: Mere conduit
provisions.content: Where an information society service is provided that consists of the transmission in a communication network of information provided by a recipient of the service, or the provision of access to a communication network, the service provider shall not be liable for the information transmitted, on condition that the provider: does not initiate the transmission; does not select the receiver of the transmission; does not select or modify the information contained in the transmission.

provisions.chapter: Chapter 4 - Liability of Intermediary Service Providers
provisions.title: Caching
provisions.content: Where an information society service is provided that consists of the transmission in a communication network of information provided by a recipient of the service, the service provider shall not be liable for the automatic, intermediate and temporary storage of that information, performed for the sole purpose of making more efficient the information's onward transmission to other recipients of the service upon their request, on condition that the provider does not modify the information and complies with conditions on access to the information.

provisions.chapter: Chapter 4 - Liability of Intermediary Service Providers
provisions.title: Hosting
provisions.content: Where an information society service is provided that consists of the storage of information provided by a recipient of the service, the service provider shall not be liable for the information stored at the request of a recipient of the service, on condition that: the provider does not have actual knowledge of illegal activity or information; as regards claims for damages, the provider is not aware of facts or circumstances from which the illegal activity or information is apparent; the provider, upon obtaining such knowledge or awareness, acts expeditiously to remove or to disable access to the information.

provisions.chapter: Chapter 4 - Liability of Intermediary Service Providers
provisions.title: No general monitoring obligation
provisions.content: Service providers shall not be obliged, when providing the services covered by Articles 13, 14 and 15, to monitor the information which they transmit or store, nor to actively seek facts or circumstances indicating illegal activity. This shall not affect targeted monitoring obligations in specific cases imposed by a court order or a decision of a competent authority in accordance with national legislation.

provisions.chapter: Chapter 4 - Liability of Intermediary Service Providers
provisions.title: Notice and takedown
provisions.content: A service provider that receives notification containing sufficient details to identify the illegal information or activity shall act expeditiously to remove or disable access to the information. Notification shall contain: identification of the notifying party; description of the content considered to be illegal; URL or location of the content; statement of good faith that the information is accurate. A provider acting in good faith upon receipt of a valid notification shall not be liable for damages.

provisions.title: Competent authority
provisions.content: The Commission for Consumer Protection and the Communications Regulation Commission shall be the competent authorities for supervision of compliance with this Act, within their respective competences. The competent authorities shall have the power to require service providers to provide information necessary for supervision, conduct inspections, and issue mandatory instructions for the removal of violations.

provisions.title: Administrative sanctions
provisions.content: A service provider who fails to provide the information required under Article 4 shall be subject to a fine of BGN 500 to BGN 3,000. A service provider who fails to comply with the requirements for commercial communications under Article 5 shall be subject to a fine of BGN 500 to BGN 5,000. For repeated violations, the fine shall be doubled.

definitions.definition: Any service normally provided for remuneration, at a distance, by electronic means and at the individual request of a recipient of services.
Any natural or legal person providing an information society service.
